title: Mizoram completes biometric enrolment of 1,679 Bangladesh refugees - The Times of India
search_term: heatwave (Aizawl OR Champhai OR Kolasib OR Lawngtlai OR Lunglei OR Mamit OR Saiha OR Serchhip) Mizoram
full_text:
Aizawl: District administration officials in
Mizoram have completed biometric enrolment of 1,679 refugees from the Chittagong Hill Tracts of Bangladesh and have finished 98.88% of the biometric enrolment of refugees from Myanmar, state home department officials said on Saturday.
The officials said the process for Bangladesh refugees was completed with enrolment of 1,361 people sheltering in Lawngtlai district, which borders Bangladesh and Myanmar. Biometric enrolment of Bangladesh refugees in Serchhip (235), Lunglei (76) and Aizawl (7) wascompleted earlier, while the Lawngtlai exercise moved slowly as the district also hosts the second-largest number of Myanmar refugees.
Refugees from Bangladesh began fleeing the Chittagong Hill Tracts in the latter part of Nov 2022 after the Bangladesh army launched a crackdown on the Kuki-Chin National Liberation Army cadres, during which civilians were also reportedly targeted.
Officials said biometric enrolment of Myanmar refugees sheltering across Mizoram’s 11 districts stood at 98.88% as of Friday. Enrolment has been completed for 29,247 refugees out of a total of 29,577 from Myanmar so far.
Seven districts — Champhai, Aizawl, Lunglei, Siaha, Mamit, Khawzawl and Saitual — have completed enrolment of Myanmar refugees, while Serchhip has finished 99.86% of the exercise.
Champhai, which hosts 12,083 Myanmar refugees, was among the first districts to complete the process.
Hnahthial district in south Mizoram, hosting 622 refugees, has completed 90.51% of enrolment.
